key_risks: Hot April PCE print May 28 lifts yields and triggers mean-reversion in high-momentum tech/Korea/Taiwan names
Semiconductor and Korea/Taiwan trades are crowded and overextended short-term; sharp factor reversal could underperform SPY
Oil shock from OPEC+ June 7 meeting or geopolitics weighs on growth/tech multiples
Stronger USD (DXY 99) pressures EWY/EWT translation returns
Weak ISM Manufacturing June 1 hits cyclical AI-linked semis disproportionately

portfolio.rationale: Strongest momentum (+6.64% 7d, +18.94% 30d) backed by Q1 chip sales +25% QoQ and massive AI capex tailwinds; SK/Taiwan strength confirms semi cycle acceleration.
Extreme momentum (+11.4% 7d, +28% 30d) tied to semiconductor cycle; trend likely to persist into June with no major adverse catalysts in window.
Momentum factor leading broad indices (+4.83% 7d, +11.86% 30d); benign macro week with no FOMC and limited high-impact data should let trends continue.
XLK +4.52% 7d with AI capex ($670B planned 2026) and 40% of S&P EPS growth from AI; durable leadership into June.
Hedge against soft consumer sentiment (UMich 44.8), sticky CPI 3.8%, and PCE release May 28 surprise risk; -3.7% 30d pullback offers entry.

portfolio_rationale: Window has limited Tier-1 US catalysts (PCE May 28, ISM Jun 1, no Fed). Riding clear semiconductor/AI momentum where SMH, EWY, EWT show parabolic trend persistence, balanced by MOMENTUM factor and a gold hedge for any hot PCE print.
rationale_summary: Lean into dominant semi/AI momentum (SMH, EWY, XLK, MTUM) which has shown overwhelming relative strength vs SPY, with a 10% gold hedge for the May 28 PCE release tail risk.
